notes.purpose: Kaggle free-tier prototype iteration for Llama-3.1-8B + QLoRA on T4x2 using the cleaned multi-source Stage 1 corpus. Not a gate-number or release artifact environment.
notes.hardware: T4x2: two discrete T4 GPUs, 16GB each. device_map='auto' makes the 8B model span both (~32GB total addressable). This config is tuned to exploit that full 32GB budget.
notes.device_placement: device_map='auto' is single-process model sharding across both GPUs — NOT data-parallel DDP. Only one process runs; model layers are spread to fit in the combined ~32GB. QLoRA+bitsandbytes 4-bit cannot use DDP (bnb.nn.Linear4bit breaks DDP gradient gathering). Do NOT use accelerate launch or torchrun with this config.
notes.dtype: T4 is Turing-class (sm_75): fp16=true, bf16=false. bfloat16 is not supported on T4. If running on A100/L40S, use the A100/general Stage 1 config instead.
notes.memory: max_seq_len=2048 plus per_device_train_batch_size=2 uses the observed T4x2 headroom without changing the effective update size. gradient_accumulation_steps=8 keeps ~32K tokens per gradient update (2048 seq_len × batch 2 × 8 accum = 32768), matching the prior batch=1/accum=16 and original seq_len=1024/accum=32 budgets while doing fewer accumulation micro-steps.
notes.checkpointing: save_steps/eval_steps=100 and save_total_limit=300 so interrupted Kaggle sessions lose less work without a separate checkpoint watcher. Disk can fill quickly; sync runs/ to persistent storage or a private HF repo before the notebook ends.
notes.path_note: Paths are config-relative for train/eval data. After cloning on Kaggle, copy or mount the off-git data/ tree under /kaggle/working/ideal-spoon/data before preflight. Required files: data/stage1/stage1.jsonl.gz and data/evals/fineweb2_haw/dev.jsonl.
notes.resume: If interrupted from repo root: PYTHONPATH=code python -m llm_hawaii.train --config code/configs/stage1_fineweb2_haw_kaggle_t4x2.json --resume-from-checkpoint runs/llama31-8b-stage1-multisource-kaggle-t4x2/checkpoint-NNN
notes.oom_fallback: If Kaggle OOMs: (1) set per_device_train_batch_size back to 1 and gradient_accumulation_steps back to 16; (2) if still OOM, reduce max_seq_len to 1024 and gradient_accumulation_steps to 32 (original conservative baseline); (3) if still OOM, reduce lora_rank to 16/lora_alpha to 32; (4) for a quick plumbing test only, try max_seq_len=512. Record the config diff in the run report.
